text.tspan: Master of Science in Computer Science, May 2026
Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering, May 2025
Developed a local LLM system using Ollama, progressing from prototype to production, including
GUI creation, RAG pipeline integration, and evaluation of larger models for performance trade-offs
Engineered a user-friendly, deployable solution, packaged for non-technical users via installer
Designed and manufactured RF circuits for communication research under Dr. Hjalti Sigmarsson
Implemented QPSK data transmission on an ADALM-PLUTO SDR in python for testing filters
Programmed microcontrollers for embedded RF systems to implement state machines and an embedded GUI
Automated testing of tunable filters on Keysight Network Analyzers through GPIB commands sent by a PC
Rotated through multiple squadrons of the 76th Software Engineering Group
Worked with software to simulate aircraft behavior and fix bugs in simulations
Analyzed circuits and programmed automatic test equipment to validate UUTs (Unit Under Test)
Learned applications of Ada, C#, and the 1553 serial data bus
Tutored OU Students in mathematics for Linear Algebra, Calculus, and Differential Equations
AI powered lock to autonomously unlock when an authorized user’s face is detected using DeepFace
Created in 24 hours using Arduino and Python, with the locking mechanism controlled by a servo motor
Winner of “Best Hardware Hack” during Hacklahoma 2023, viewable at https://devpost.com/software/faceguard
Using 2 focused Yagi Antennas mounted to servos, this project maintains a wifi connection over long ranges while moving
ESP32s control the servos and communicate GPS through the wifi network. The front end is built in Python with Tkinter
Reprogrammed the ECU firmware in C to send telemetry CAN messages and validate faults
Designed the sooner racing team website, viewable at soonerracing.com
Started the EV FSAE team at OU and designed the EV Accumulator (battery) and shutdown circuit
Designed and implemented the electronic throttle control system on the IC car according to FSAE rules
Added custom telemetry sensors to the car for coolant flow rate, temperature, suspension travel, and more
Collected and interpreted live voltage signals from a dynamometer’s load cell for torque and the ECU’s RPM
tachometer output through National Instruments DAQ devices in Python
Implemented the Short Time Fourier Transform to analyze live analog waveforms (representing RPM)
Designed and constructed the circuit for the electric bidirectional solenoid shifting mechanism on the IC car.
Analog & Digital Circuit Design, Object Oriented Programming, Computer Networks, Database Management, Software
